catalog_content:
Item Name: Community Coffee Medium Roast Single Serve KCup Compatible Pods Box of Pods, Breakfast Blend, 18 Count
Bullet Point 1: BREAKFAST BLEND: Start your day with this smooth, bright, medium roast coffee with notes of honey & hazelnut. Compatible with single-serve brewing systems, including Keurig 2.0 k-cup brewers.
Bullet Point 2: COFFEE PODS: We take high-quality 100% Arabica beans, roast & grind them to perfection, then package them in single-serve coffee pods that are Keurig 2.0 (k-cup) compatible for quick & easy brewing.
Bullet Point 3: COMMUNITY IS EVERYTHING: From using responsibly-sourced coffee beans to programs that support military service members, local schools & more, Community Coffee gives back to our partners & local communities in every way we can.
Bullet Point 4: HOW YOU LIKE IT: We have k-cup compatible single-serve coffee pods for a quickly brewed cup. For reusable pods, we have whole beans or ground coffee for the same great Community Coffee taste.
Bullet Point 5: QUALITY ASSURED: All of our coffee beans go through a rigorous scoring system for body, balance, flavor & aroma. Only then can they be considered fit to bear the Community Coffee name.
Value: 18.0
Unit: count

overall_summary: Single-serve coffee pods from a family-owned brand, positioned as a mid-range product with a focus on quality and community involvement. Pricing is likely driven by convenience, brand trust, and the use of 100% Arabica beans, though no premium or organic certifications are visible.

Product Description: Homeopathic medicines are therapeutically active micro-doses of mineral, botanical and biological substances. They are reliable and safe drugs, therefore considered by many as an excellent first choice when self-medicating. Homeopathy has been used for more than 200 years, building a remarkable safety record and generating a great body of knowledge. Today, homeopathy is used by millions of patients in more than 65 countries and recommended by more than 400,000 healthcare providers. Since the passing of the federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act in 1938, homeopathic medicines have been regulated as drugs by the United States Food and Drug Administration (FDA).1 To be accepted as a homeopathic medicine by the FDA, product ingredients must be identified in the Homeopathic Pharmacopoeia of the United States (HPUS) or generally accepted as homeopathic under the principles of homeopathy.
